feature_name: Credit balance refund to customer's bank account as per RBI guidelines

cs_strategy.title: Refund Status Tracker
cs_strategy.summary: Build in-app status tracking showing consent capture confirmation, processing stage, expected completion date, and failure reasons with retry options.
cs_strategy.rationale: Addresses the critical PRD gap where users have no visibility after giving consent, preventing high-likelihood contacts about refund status and failed transfers.

differentiation.title: Automated Refund Processing
differentiation.summary: Build fully automated credit balance refund system that eliminates manual documentation requirements and processes refunds within RBI's 7-day compliance window.
differentiation.rationale: Competitors like SBI Card rely on manual, documentation-heavy processes while digital-first players haven't differentiated on this feature, creating opportunity for automation advantage.

prd:
# PRD: Automated Credit Balance Refund System

**Version:** 1.0  
**Author:** Associate Director, Products  
**Last Updated:** [Date]  
**Status:** Draft for VP Review

---

## 1. Strategic Context (Why Now)

RBI mandates that customers must be offered the option to receive credit balances from billed transaction refunds into their bank account within 7 days, failing which the amount adjusts against outstanding. Non-compliance exposes PIXEL to regulatory penalties and customer complaints to the Banking Ombudsman. Manual processing of these refunds creates operational overhead, delays beyond the 7-day window, and poor customer experience that drives negative app store reviews and social media escalations.

**Opportunity cost of delay:** Every quarter without automation means continued manual processing costs, regulatory risk exposure, and competitive disadvantage as neo-banks and fintech-first issuers launch automated refund experiences that position traditional card issuers as legacy players.

---

## 2. Persona Definition

### Persona 1: High-Frequency E-commerce Shopper
**Who they are:** Salaried professional, 25-34 years, Tier 1/2 cities, spends ₹35-60k/month on card, 8-12 online transactions monthly across Flipkart, Amazon, Swiggy, Zomato, fashion platforms.

**Primary behaviour today:** Returns 2-3 items per month due to size/quality issues; receives refunds 5-15 days after return pickup; checks credit card statement multiple times to verify refund posting; calls customer care when refund doesn't reflect within expected timeline.

**Specific pain point:** When a refund posts after bill generation but before payment, they've already paid that amount from their bank account, creating a credit balance. They want this money back in their account immediately, not adjusted against next month's bill, because it impacts their monthly cash flow planning.

**Adoption trigger:** Automatic notification when credit balance is created with one-tap option to transfer to bank account, money received within 2-3 working days without calling customer care or filling forms.

---

### Persona 2: Premium Spender with Large Transactions
**Who they are:** Self-employed professional or business owner, 35-50 years, metro cities, spends ₹1.5-4L/month on card, makes high-value purchases (electronics, travel bookings, business expenses).

**Primary behaviour today:** Books flight/hotel packages worth ₹80k-2L; cancels or modifies bookings due to plan changes; receives refunds of ₹50k-1.5L that create substantial credit balances; expects immediate access to their money given the large amounts involved.

**Specific pain point:** Credit balance of ₹1L+ sitting on card for 30-45 days until next billing cycle feels like an interest-free loan to the bank; they want immediate liquidity, especially for business cash flow; current process requires branch visit or multiple customer care calls with 7-10 day processing time.

**Adoption trigger:** Proactive alert for credit balances above ₹25k with instant refund initiation; clear visibility of refund status and expected credit date; no documentation or follow-up required.

---

### Persona 3: Budget-Conscious First-Time Credit Card User
**Who they are:** Young professional or student, 22-28 years, Tier 2/3 cities, spends ₹8-18k/month on card, new to credit (card vintage <18 months), maintains low credit utilization.

**Primary behaviour today:** Uses card cautiously; pays full amount before due date to avoid interest; gets confused when statement shows credit balance after a refund; doesn't understand why they can't access this money immediately; fears hidden charges or complications.

**Specific pain point:** Lacks understanding of credit balance mechanics; worries that credit balance might expire or get lost; doesn't know how to request refund to bank account; intimidated by calling customer care or visiting branch; wants simple, app-based solution with clear instructions.

**Adoption trigger:** In-app education when credit balance is created explaining what it means; simple yes/no choice presented in vernacular language; confirmation message showing money will reach their account with specific date; no jargon or complex steps.

---

## 3. Problem Statement

When customers receive refunds for billed transactions after paying their credit card bill, the resulting credit balance remains locked on their card for 30-45 days until the next billing cycle, forcing them to either wait or navigate a manual, documentation-heavy process involving customer care calls and 7-10 day processing times to get their money back. This creates cash flow friction, generates 15-20% of total customer care call volume related to refunds, and violates RBI's mandate to offer bank account refund option within 7 days. The manual process exposes PIXEL to regulatory non-compliance risk, operational costs of ₹150-200 per refund request, and customer dissatisfaction that manifests in app ratings below 4.0 for refund-related reviews.

---

## 4. Current State Metrics (Baseline)

| Metric | Current Baseline | Data Owner |
|--------|------------------|------------|
| Monthly credit balance instances (refunds of billed transactions) | Unknown — measurement needed | Card Operations Team |
| Average credit balance amount | Unknown — measurement needed | Card Operations Team |
| % of credit balances >₹1,000 | Unknown — measurement needed | Card Operations Team |
| % of credit balances >₹10,000 | Unknown — measurement needed | Card Operations Team |
| Customer-initiated refund requests per month | Unknown — measurement needed | Customer Support Team |
| Average processing time for manual refund requests | 7-10 days (known from CS SLA) | Customer Support Team |
| Customer care calls related to credit balance/refunds | Unknown — measurement needed | Customer Support Team |
| % of refund requests completed within RBI's 7-day window | Unknown — measurement needed | Compliance Team |
| Cost per manual refund request (CS + Ops) | Unknown — measurement needed | Finance Team |
| App store reviews mentioning refund issues (1-3 star) | Unknown — measurement needed | Product Analytics Team |
| Credit balance aging: % adjusted vs % refunded | Unknown — measurement needed | Card Operations Team |

---

## 5. Scope

### In Scope
- Detection of credit balance created by refund of billed transaction
- In-app notification to customer when credit balance is detected
- In-app consent capture interface with 7-day countdown timer
- Customer selection of destination bank account from linked accounts
- Automated NEFT/IMPS transfer initiation to customer's bank account
- Real-time status tracking: consent captured → processing → funds transferred → completed
- SMS and push notification at each status change
- Failure handling with specific error messages and retry option
- Auto-adjustment to outstanding balance if no response within 7 days
- Audit trail of consent, transfer initiation, and completion for regulatory reporting
- CS dashboard showing all refund requests with status and failure reasons

### Out of Scope
- **Refunds of unbilled transactions:** RBI guideline applies only to refunds of billed transactions; unbilled refunds automatically adjust against current cycle outstanding, no customer action needed.
- **Instant refunds (real-time transfer):** MVP uses NEFT with T+1 settlement; instant refunds via IMPS require additional banking partner integration and will be evaluated for V2 based on customer demand and cost-benefit analysis.
- **Refund to different bank account than registered:** Security and fraud risk; customer must use bank accounts already linked and verified in PIXEL app; adding new account for refund requires separate account linking flow (out of scope for this PRD).
- **Partial refund requests:** Customer cannot request refund of partial credit balance amount; entire credit balance must be refunded or adjusted; partial refunds create reconciliation complexity and will be considered post-MVP based on user research.
- **Proactive refund without consent:** RBI requires explicit customer consent; cannot auto-refund without user action even if it seems customer-friendly; consent capture is mandatory compliance requirement.

---

## 6. User Stories with Acceptance Criteria

### Story 1: High-Frequency E-commerce Shopper — Credit Balance Notification and Consent
**As a High-Frequency E-commerce Shopper, I want to receive immediate notification when a refund creates a credit balance on my card so that I can decide within 7 days whether to transfer it to my bank account or adjust it against my next bill.**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**AC1:** Given I have a credit balance of ₹500 or more created by a refund of a billed transaction, when the refund posts to my account, then I receive a push notification within 2 hours stating "You have a credit balance of ₹[amount]. Transfer to your bank account or adjust against next bill? Decide within 7 days" with a deep link to the refund consent screen.

**AC2:** Given I tap the notification and land on the refund consent screen, when the screen loads, then I see: (a) credit balance amount in ₹, (b) source of credit balance (merchant name and refund date), (c) two clear options: "Transfer to Bank Account" and "Adjust Against Outstanding", (d) countdown timer showing "X days Y hours remaining to decide", (e) explanation text: "If no action is taken, amount will be adjusted against your outstanding balance after 7 days as per RBI guidelines."

**AC3:** Given I select "Transfer to Bank Account" and tap Continue, when I proceed, then I see a list of my linked bank accounts with account number (last 4 digits visible), bank name, and account holder name, with a radio button to select one account.

**Failure-state AC:** Given the credit balance detection service fails to trigger notification within 2 hours of refund posting, when the system detects this failure in the next batch run (every 6 hours), then I receive a delayed notification with the same content and the countdown timer adjusted to reflect actual remaining time, and an alert is logged to the monitoring dashboard for engineering review.

---

### Story 2: Premium Spender with Large Transactions — Refund Initiation and Status Tracking
**As a Premium Spender with Large Transactions, I want to initiate a refund to my bank account with one tap and track its status in real-time so that I know exactly when the money will reach my account without calling customer care.**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**AC1:** Given I have selected my destination bank account and tapped "Confirm Refund", when the system processes my consent, then I see a confirmation screen showing: (a) "Refund Initiated" status, (b) refund amount, (c) destination bank account (masked), (d) expected credit date (T+2 working days from initiation), (e) unique refund reference number, (f) option to "Track Status" that takes me to the refund tracker screen.

**AC2:** Given my refund is being processed, when I navigate to the refund tracker screen (accessible from home screen "Recent Activity" or dedicated "Refunds" section in menu), then I see a progress stepper with 4 stages: (1) Consent Captured [completed, with timestamp], (2) Processing [current stage, with "In Progress" label], (3) Transferred to Bank [pending], (4) Completed [pending], and below the stepper I see "Expected completion: [date]" and "Refund reference: [number]".

**AC3:** Given my refund has been successfully transferred, when the bank confirms credit to my account, then the refund tracker updates to show stage 4 "Completed" with timestamp, I receive a push notification "₹[amount] refund credited to your account ending [last 4 digits]", and an SMS confirmation with the same details.

**Failure-state AC:** Given the NEFT transfer fails due to invalid account details or bank rejection, when the failure is detected, then the refund tracker shows stage 2 "Processing" with a red error badge, displays specific error message ("Transfer failed: Invalid account number" or "Transfer failed: Bank rejected transaction"), shows a "Retry" button that takes me back to bank account selection screen, and I receive a push notification "Refund failed. Please update bank account details and retry. Amount will be adjusted against outstanding if not completed within [X] days."

---

### Story 3: Budget-Conscious First-Time Credit Card User — Educational Guidance and Simple Choice
**As a Budget-Conscious First-Time Credit Card User, I want clear explanation of what credit balance means and simple guidance on what to do so that I can make an informed decision without confusion or fear of hidden charges.**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**AC1:** Given I am a first-time user encountering credit balance (identified by card vintage <12 months and first credit balance instance), when I land on the refund consent screen, then I see an educational tooltip or expandable section at the top with heading "What is Credit Balance?" and explanation: "You paid ₹[amount] on your bill, but received a refund of ₹[amount] after payment. This creates a credit balance. You can: (1) Transfer it to your bank account (no charges), or (2) Use it to reduce your next bill. Your choice." in simple language (English and Hindi toggle available).

**AC2:** Given I am viewing the two refund options, when I tap on "Transfer to Bank Account" or "Adjust Against Outstanding", then I see a brief description below each option: "Transfer to Bank Account: Money will reach your account in 2-3 working days. No charges." and "Adjust Against Outstanding: Amount will reduce your next bill. No action needed from you."

**AC3:** Given I have selected "Transfer to Bank Account" and confirmed my bank account, when the refund is initiated, then I receive an SMS in simple language: "Your credit card refund of Rs.[amount] is being processed. Money will reach your [Bank Name] account ending [last 4 digits] by [date]. Track status in PIXEL app. No charges applied."

**Failure-state AC:** Given I do not take any action within 7 days, when the 7-day window expires, then the credit balance is automatically adjusted against my outstanding balance, I receive a notification "Your credit balance of ₹[amount] has been adjusted against your outstanding balance as you did not respond within 7 days. Your new outstanding: ₹[amount]", and the transaction appears in my statement as "Credit Balance Adjustment" with the date.

---

### Story 4: High-Frequency E-commerce Shopper — Multiple Credit Balances Management
**As a High-Frequency E-commerce Shopper, I want to view all my pending credit balances in one place and take action on multiple refunds together so that I don't have to respond to each notification separately.**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**AC1:** Given I have 2 or more pending credit balances awaiting consent, when I navigate to the "Refunds" section in the app menu, then I see a list of all pending credit balances with: (a) merchant name, (b) refund amount, (c) refund date, (d) days remaining to decide (countdown), (e) "Take Action" button for each item, sorted by days remaining (urgent first).

**AC2:** Given I am viewing the list of pending credit balances, when I tap "Take Action" on any item, then I am taken to the refund consent screen for that specific credit balance with pre-filled details and the same flow as Story 1.

**AC3:** Given I have completed action (either transfer or adjust) on a credit balance, when I return to the "Refunds" section, then that item moves from "Pending Action" tab to "Completed" tab, showing status (Transferred/Adjusted), completion date, and refund reference number if transferred.

**Failure-state AC:** Given the system fails to load the list of pending credit balances due to backend service downtime, when I navigate to the "Refunds" section, then I see an error message "Unable to load refunds. Please try again later." with a "Retry" button, and the error is logged to monitoring dashboard with alert to on-call engineer if downtime exceeds 15 minutes.

---

### Story 5: Premium Spender with Large Transactions — Refund Failure Recovery
**As a Premium Spender with Large Transactions, I want clear guidance and quick resolution when my refund fails so that I can get my money within the 7-day compliance window without escalating to customer care.**

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**AC1:** Given my refund transfer failed due to a correctable error (invalid account, bank rejection), when I tap "Retry" on the failure screen, then I am taken to the bank account selection screen with: (a) the previously selected account marked with a red error icon and error message, (b) option to select a different linked account, (c) option to "Add New Bank Account" (which triggers standard account linking flow), (d) "Need Help?" link that opens in-app chat with CS agent with refund reference pre-filled.

**AC2:** Given I have selected a different bank account and tapped "Confirm Refund" again, when the system processes the retry, then a new refund attempt is initiated with a new refund reference number, the countdown timer for the 7-day window continues from the original credit balance creation date (not reset),
